messages.content: I bought Remote Start a couple of days ago and it still won't work. The car has no signal where it is parked. The subscription itself shows active though. My car has been parked in an underground garage all week.

reference:
<think>
The cues in the ticket are: car has no signal; subscription shows active; parked in underground garage all week. Subscription is active but the TCU lost cellular signal, so the vehicle never received the push. so my leading hypothesis is TCU_OFFLINE. I also considered ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE, but cache issues show an inconsistent active state, not a car-side timeout, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the TCU last_seen status from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.80 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

messages.content: I subscribed to Vehicle Finder 3 days ago and now it's acting up. It worked fine yesterday and then suddenly stopped. I can see it active on the website but the app shows it as off. It's intermittent and flickers on and off.

reference:
<think>
The cues in the ticket are: worked fine yesterday then stopped; active on the website but the app shows off; intermittent and flickers. Entitlement was created but the app/CCS cache has not refreshed, so the app shows the feature as missing. so my leading hypothesis is ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE. I also considered TCU_OFFLINE, but a TCU offline issue shows a car-side timeout, not an app showing the feature missing, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the cache invalidation status from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.81 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

messages.content: I paid for Remote Climate 3 days ago but I can't use it. The app keeps prompting me to Subscribe even though I already paid. It has been like this since I paid, every single time.

reference:
<think>
The cues in the ticket are: app prompting subscribe even though paid; since paid every single time. The webhook arrived but eligibility rejected the region/trim/plan combo, so no entitlement was created. so my leading hypothesis is ELIGIBILITY_RULE_CONFLICT. I also considered ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE, but cache stale would still show the entitlement active somewhere; here nothing is active, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the eligibility decision log from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.73 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

Payment went through, but the payment-to-entitlement event did not complete, so the entitlement was never created. so my leading hypothesis is PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_LOST. I also considered ELIGIBILITY_RULE_CONFLICT, but an eligibility block is a persistent reject, not a brand-new purchase still settling, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the payment capture or activation-event status from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.82 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.

The entitlement exists, but the IAM token/scope was not refreshed, so the feature returns a 403 / permission error. so my leading hypothesis is TOKEN_SCOPE. I also considered ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE, but a stale cache shows a wrong status, it does not throw a permission error, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the token's scope claims or expiry from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.72 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

<think>
The cues in the ticket are: charge still processing; hours ago, still not activated; bought a few minutes ago, nothing switched on. Payment went through, but the payment-to-entitlement event did not complete, so the entitlement was never created. so my leading hypothesis is PAYMENT_WEBHOOK_LOST. I also considered ELIGIBILITY_RULE_CONFLICT, but an eligibility block is a persistent reject, not a brand-new purchase still settling, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the payment capture or activation-event status from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.82 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

messages.content: Here's the trouble I'm hitting with the remote feature. I can sign in to the app fine for everything else. Opening the feature throws a 403 permission denied.

reference:
<think>
The cues in the ticket are: sign in fine for everything else; 403 permission denied on the feature. The entitlement exists, but the IAM token/scope was not refreshed, so the feature returns a 403 / permission error. so my leading hypothesis is TOKEN_SCOPE. I also considered ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE, but a stale cache shows a wrong status, it does not throw a permission error, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the token's scope claims or expiry from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.73 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

messages.content: I bought Remote Lock/Unlock yesterday and it still won't work. When I tap it in the app it just spins and then times out. The car has no signal where it is parked. My car has been parked in an underground garage all week.

reference:
<think>
The cues in the ticket are: the app spins and times out; car has no signal; parked in underground garage all week. Subscription is active but the TCU lost cellular signal, so the vehicle never received the push. so my leading hypothesis is TCU_OFFLINE. I also considered ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE, but cache issues show an inconsistent active state, not a car-side timeout, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the TCU last_seen status from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.82 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.
</think>

reference:

I also considered TCU_OFFLINE, but a TCU offline issue shows a car-side timeout, not an app showing the feature missing, so I down-weight it. I cannot see the cache invalidation status from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.82 and flag what a human should verify. Meanwhile I give the customer a step-by-step self-triage, fastest first: a quick app refresh rules out a stale cache, getting the car into open signal rules out or fixes a connectivity issue, and if neither helps it points to a server-side activation problem we handle.

messages.content: I paid for the connected services subscription a few days ago but I can't use it. Why does it still ask me to buy when I already paid? The app keeps prompting me to Subscribe even though I already paid. I have a CR-V Touring here in US-West.

reference:
<think>
The cues in the ticket are: ask me to buy already paid; app prompting subscribe even though paid; cr v touring in us west. The webhook arrived but eligibility rejected the region/trim/plan combo, so no entitlement was created. so my leading hypothesis is ELIGIBILITY_RULE_CONFLICT. I cannot see the token's scope claims or expiry from the ticket, so I keep confidence around 0.81 and flag what a human should verify.
